entry.title: How to send an E-Mail Copy when Submitting a Sales Quote
entry.content:
We would like to get a copy of the generated e-mail from C4C.
Especially, the Sales Quote, when the user hits the submit button.
It should be the send message in msg Format, as you normal have it in the sent folder of an e-mail-client.
Is there a way to achieve this?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to send an E-Mail Copy when Submitting a Sales Quote
entry.content:
There is no general functionality to send e-mail copies for all types of business documents, but there is an option to send e-mail copies for sales quotes. 

Before you submit a sales quote, you can maintain an e-mail recipient to whom the copy is sent. 

  1. Edit the Sales Quote
  2. Select Actions > Edit Output Settings
  3. Select Send By settings: E-Mail (if not yet maintained) 
  4. In the E-Mail Settings, maintain a row with the 'To' prefix and the E-Mail address of the main recipient (if not yet maintained) 
  5. Select Add Row, maintain the 'CC' prefix and the E-Mail address of the recipient who shall receive a copy
  6. Apply and Save

This is the only option.

A copy of the generated e-mail can only be defined for each individual sales quote.

entry.title: Output management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Output management in S/4HANA

Introduction to output management

After working with many clients in different fields, I've noticed that most businesses struggle with output management. Each client has their own idea of what output means, whether it's PDF documents, forms, emails, or iDocs. While these views are somewhat correct, I think output management is about effectively communicating with customers or vendors using the right channels and documents.

When we talk about output management, I see it as combining "output" (like print, email, iDoc) with "management" (how we set it up to reuse the functionality). Output management in SAP S/4HANA is a big improvement in how businesses generate and distribute documents. This new framework solves the complexities and inefficiencies of the many old output solutions in previous SAP systems. This blog will mostly cover the output management features in on-premises S/4HANA.

Legacy Output Solutions

Historically, SAP provided several output management tools tailored to specific business areas:

  • SD Output Control (NAST): Used primarily for sales and distribution processes.
  • FI Correspondence: Handles financial document communications.
  • FI-CA Print Workbench: Manages print jobs for contract accounts receivable and payable.
  • CRM Post-Processing Framework (PPF): Deals with post-processing activities in customer relationship management.

While these tools were effective, they required customers to learn and maintain distinct frameworks for each business area. This led to a high total cost of ownership (TCO) and varied user experiences.

What is NAST

NAST is a central component in SAP for managing and controlling the output of documents. It is part of the SAP Output Control framework and is used primarily in the Sales and Distribution (SD) module, but its functionality extends to other areas such as Materials Management (MM) and Financial Accounting (FI).

Key features of NAST include:

  • Message control
  • Output type
  • Condition record
  • Processing routine
  • Transmission medium
  • Scheduling

NAST operates using the condition technique, which is the traditional method of working in SAP.

The Need for a Unified Solution

In SAP S/4HANA projects, the fragmentation of output management tools presented several challenges:

  • Complexity: Customers had to navigate and master different frameworks for different tasks.
  • Maintenance: Each framework required separate setup and ongoing maintenance.
  • User Experience: Disparate tools resulted in inconsistent user experiences across the SAP ecosystem.

What is Output Management in S/4HANA?

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA is a comprehensive framework designed to streamline the creation, formatting, and distribution of business documents. It modernizes and centralizes outdated output mechanisms, effectively meeting the diverse needs of various business processes.

Key features of S/4HANA Output Management include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Offers a single point of configuration for all output types, reducing complexity and easing maintenance. This common setup point can be used across different modules.
  • Flexibility and Scalability: Supports various output channels and formats, making it adaptable to diverse business needs and scalable for future growth.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Provides consistent and intuitive user interfaces, improving efficiency and satisfaction across different modules.
  • Simplified Maintenance: Eliminates the need for complex master data maintenance and extensive cutover activities.
  • Advanced Output Channels: Supports modern output channels like email, print, fax, and electronic data interchange (EDI), in addition to traditional methods.
  • Improved Template Management: Utilizes advanced tools for designing and managing document templates, such as Adobe Forms, enhancing document consistency and branding.

Different between NAST and Output management in S/4HAN

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

NAST is the traditional output control functionality used in earlier versions of SAP, such as SAP ECC. It manages the output of business documents through various channels like print, email, fax, and EDI. Key characteristics of NAST include:

  • Decentralized Configuration: Each application module (e.g., SD, MM, FI) has its own configuration, leading to complexity and redundancy.
  • Limited Flexibility: Customization and enhancement of output processes require significant effort and often involve custom ABAP development.
  • Condition Records: Utilizes condition records to determine when and how outputs are triggered.
  • Processing Routines: Depends on traditional forms like SAP script and Smart Forms for document formatting.
  • Separate Handling: Each business area (e.g., sales orders, invoices) has its own output determination and processing logic
  • It also covers Multiple Output Mediums and Error Handling and Monitoring

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

The new Output Management framework in SAP S/4HANA is designed to overcome the limitations of NAST and provide a more streamlined and efficient approach. Its key features include:

  • Centralized Configuration: Provides a unified setup for all output types, reducing complexity and maintenance efforts.
  • Enhanced Flexibility: Supports various output channels and formats with easier customization options.
  • Advanced Template Management: Uses Adobe Forms for designing and managing document templates, offering more sophisticated formatting and branding capabilities.
  • Output Parameter Determination: Utilizes a modern method for determining output parameters based on business context, simplifying the output determination process.
  • Improved User Experience: Consistent and user-friendly interfaces across different modules enhance operational efficiency.
  • Integration: Seamlessly integrates with other SAP S/4HANA components and external systems, improving overall document management and processing.

Key Differences

Feature

NAST (Legacy Output Control)

Output Management in SAP S/4HANA

Configuration

Decentralized, specific to each module

Centralized, unified for all output types

Flexibility

Limited, requires custom ABAP development

Enhanced, easier customization

Forms and Templates

SAP script, Smart Forms

Adobe Forms

Output Parameter Determination

Condition records

Modern, context-based parameter determination

User Experience

Inconsistent across modules

Consistent and user-friendly

Integration

Separate handling for each business area

Seamless integration with S/4HANA components

Maintenance

Complex, higher total cost of ownership (TCO)

Simplified, lower TCO

 

Advantages and features of Output management

SAP S/4 HANA Output Management has the below features

  • Operates seamlessly in both cloud and on-premises environments.
  • Offers a unified solution for all output related tasks across the product and modules
    Reduce master data maintenance and relatively less time consuming for activation
  • Supports standard extensibility for configuration documents
  • Offers central monitoring tools for key users, enhancing oversight and control.

Based on Business Rule Framework (BRF), offering greater flexibility in managing output processes.

How it works?

For determining output types in transactions, we can utilize Output Parameter Determination (OPD). Maintenance of OPD can function similarly to master data or be set up via transport management. I recommend using OPD as a client-specific change. Using transports can lead to confusion, especially if other rollouts are occurring simultaneously.

Prerequisites –

There are few back-end changes you need to do it before maintenance of OPD setup.

  • Check if your application object type is active
  • Check if your Output type is available and if you want to use your own output then create new one by doing copy of standard output type.
  • Assign channel to your Output type.
  • Assign Form Template to Output Type
  • Assign Email template to output type.

To use Output management, we need to activate the required object and if we activate Output management then system will redirect output always to Output management and not to NAST.

 

Main parameters in OPD (Output Parameter Determination)

Below are the key parameters,

Output management is BRF based functionality which can be setup by using below 7 steps in the SAP using OPD.

Let’s check in the system –

OPD (Output parameter determination) screen

There are multiple steps (as per above screenshot) which should be maintained to determine output in billing document as per this example, here we will take billing output type as an example.

1. Output Type –

This step begins by deciding whether to process the output immediately or later via a job. As shown below, for the combination of billing type F2 and output type BILLING_DOCUMENT, we can choose to process the output type immediately or through a background job (VF31F) at the time of billing document creation. Additionally, in the billing document, we can manually add the output type and set the dispatch time manually.

2. Receiver –

In this step, we can determine the output type for a specific role (partner function). If we have four lines with different roles and want to consider only the first two, we will mark the Exclusive indicator 'X' for the second line. This way, the system will only consider the first two entries and ignore any entries after the second line.

3. Channel –

In this stage, the 'Channel' will be defined. The channel could be IDoc, Print, Email, etc. This stage will also include an exclusive indicator.

For instance, if the exclusive indicator 'X' is set for IDoc as shown below, the system will not search for or determine the PRINT and EMAIL channels.

4. Printer setting –

In this phase, we can specify the printer and the number of copies to be printed after creating the document. Before setting up the printer in this phase, it is necessary to install the printer in SAP.

Additionally, please be aware that the new output management system supports Printer Spool Devices limited to PDF device types only (PDFUC: PDF Unicode 1.3 / PDF1: PDF ISO Latin1 4.60+).

This restriction is detailed under the Symptoms section in note 268480 and SAP Note. 2294198

5. Email setting –

In this phase, the sender's email ID can be configured. This means that when an email is sent to the customer, the sender's email will be set up here, and the recipient's email will be the customer's email address.

Additionally, we can also establish the email template in this phase. An email template specifies the format and content that the customer will receive from us via email.

6. Email recipient –

The email address (recipient address) retrieved in the billing document is sourced from BP master data or determined through this step.

If you have configured an email address here, this setting takes precedence.

Multiple email addresses can be maintained in this determination step by separating them with ';'.
You can also define the email type code with TO, CC, BCC.

7. Form Template –

This step is intended for maintaining the form template used for each output. Ensure that the form template maintained here has been assigned to the output type in SPRO -> Cross-Application Components -> Output Control -> Assign Form Templates.

If the language is not specified in OPD, it will be retrieved from the customer master data of the output recipient.

8. Output Relevance –

This step verifies if the output item is flagged as relevant for output.

If the relevance indicator is not set to 'X - true', the output item determined in the billing document will not be considered relevant for output.

Consequently, its status will remain 'In preparation' in the billing document under the output section.

OPD functionalities –

Adding extra predefined standard fields

Adding new columns or fields: OPD provides default fields that cover all necessary steps we've discussed. However, often after client discussions, we realize additional parameters are needed to define our objects in OPD differently.

In OPD, there are additional fields available that we can add as columns on the main OPD screen. These can then be utilized in determination combinations as needed.

To do this, navigate to Edit -> Table settings -> Insert column -> From context data object -> Search.

Click on search

Following this step, a list of standard additional fields will be available for us to add and utilize in OPD. If the required field is still not available, we can proceed with an enhancement to add the necessary field to this list. This process requires the involvement of an ABAP specialist.

Simulation functionality

Having reviewed all seven steps, we can use them to determine the output type in the transaction.

To preview the output in simulation mode, use the highlighted option below. This allows us to simulate the output effectively.

Key points and learnings –

Pros

  • As OPD is BRF based functionality, we can import and export data using predefined template.
  • New column (fields) can be added and can be removed as per requirement.
  • SAP S/4HANA output management is recommended but not mandatory.
  • The scope of SAP S/4HANA output management is not comparable with the scope of NAST.
  • SAP S/4HANA output control is not designed to completely replace NAST.
  • Innovations will only be made in the new framework (SAP S/4HANA output management)

Cons –

  • It will only cover the output part of NAST (print, email, partly EDI).
  • The rest – like system integration, workflow, or special function – is not (and will not be) in the scope of SAP S/4HANA output control.
  • Customers will lose all NAST features when switching to SAP S/4HANA Output Control. Using S/4HANA Output Control for applications that previously relied on NAST is only advisable in exceptional cases
  • iDocs are not fully supported by the new output management. There are few restrictions as compared to NAST.
  • NO ALE is supported in Output management.
  • No tool is available for conversion from NAST to Output management.

Reference –

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/output-management-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13492419

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/d7bd4b5d70d94f09bcf99b221a7a1688.html

Check out SAP notes related to the topic -

  • SAP Note 2292539 - SAP S/4HANA output control – configuration
  • 2843926 - It is not possible to activate rules in App 'Output Parameter Determination' - SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Note 2228611 –Output Management in S/4HANA 
  • SAP Note 2248229 –SAP S/4HANA output control - download XML file
  • SAP Note 2292571 –SAP S/4HANA output control -technical setup 
  • SAP Note 2292646 –SAP S/4HANA output control -form templates with fragments 
  • SAP Note 2292681 –SAP S/4HANA output control –master form templates 
  • SAP Note 2294198 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized forms 
  • SAP Note 2367080 –SAP S/4HANA output control -customized master forms

entry.title: Re: Valuing dimensions with members # using already avail...
entry.content:
hi @andreag412 ,

to achieve your goal, you would need to. Aggregate data into a variables - the dimensions you want to merge (like Customer, Channel, Sales...), Next you put IF statement to process only rows with Net Sales and overwrite measure values with the aggregated ones. Something like that

VARIABLEMEMBER #Customer OF [d/Customer]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Channel  OF [d/Channel]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Scat 	 OF [d/SalesCat]
VARIABLEMEMBER #IC       OF [d/Intercompany]

DATA([d/Cusomter] = #Customer,
	 [d/Channel] = #Channel,
	 [d/Scat] = #Scat,
	 [d/Intercompany] = #IC) = RESULTLOOKUP()

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="NetSales") != NULL THEN
	DATA([d/Measures] = "StdCost") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measure] = "StdCost"
												  [d/Cusomter] = #Customer,
												  [d/Channel] = #Channel,
												  [d/Scat] = #Scat,
												  [d/Intercompany] = #IC)
	DATA([d/Measures] = "StdCostMark") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measure] = "StdCostMark"
												  [d/Cusomter] = #Customer,
												  [d/Channel] = #Channel,
												  [d/Scat] = #Scat,
												  [d/Intercompany] = #IC)	
ENDIF

 I'm not sure if this code will work, just drafted it. But it can be achieved this way.

entry.title: How to Import Data from DataBricks Spark on AWS to SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome to our comprehensive guide on setting up a JDBC connection between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Databricks. Whether you are using the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit or opting for a manual setup, this tutorial will walk you through each step to ensure a smooth and successful integration. By following these detailed instructions, you'll be able to download and install the necessary JDBC drivers, create and configure the required property files, and establish a secure connection to your Databricks SQL warehouses. Let's dive in and get started on enhancing your data analytics capabilities with SAC and Databricks.

Prerequisite

Performed setup using the SAP Analytics Cloud Agent Simple Deployment Kit or

Performed a manual setup by following the steps 1,2,4,5 in Prerequisites

1. Download and Install JDBC Driver

a. Download the latest JDBC drive from databricks.com All JDBC Driver Versions

b. Extract the zip file and then place the downloaded jar file(s) in an appropriate folder. The jar files can be in any location on your machine as long as the properties file includes the correct file locations. For example, 

2. Create a Property File

a. Create a properties file that specifies the paths to JDBC drivers you want to use, and place it in the same file system where the SAP Analytics Cloud agent is installed. The properties file can have any name, such as DriverConfig.properties. See Example of a properties file

b. Open the file as an administrator and update line from 

#Generic JDBC datasource="path_to_JDBCdriver"

to 

Generic JDBC datasource=C:\Program Files\SAP\SACAgentKit\drivers\DatabricksJDBC42-2.6.36.1062\DatabricksJDBC42.jar

Note: C:\Program Files\SAP\SACAgentKit\drivers\DatabricksJDBC42-2.6.36.1062\DatabricksJDBC42.jar is the path I placed the jar file in the Blog. You have to change it to the real path where you place the jar file in your system

c. Save the property file 

d. Restart Tomcat

3. Find Connection Information in DataBricks

a. JDBC URL

Login DataBricks on AWS > Click SQL Warehouses from the navigational menu> Select SQL warehouses tab > Select a target SQL warehouse name

Click on Connection details tab within the target warehouse > Copy all strings from the JDBC URL box

b. Based on the DataBricks official document, “by default, the connector uses the schema named default and authenticates the connection using the user name token”

c. Based on the DataBricks official document, JDBC Class name is com.databricks.client.jdbc.Driver

d. Find password.

Click on the user profile > select User Settings

Select Developer tab from the Settings area > Click on Manage button in the Access tokens section

Click on Generate new token button

Input a meaningful name > set Lifetime days for the token (maximum 10 years) > Click on  Generate > Copy the token string > Store in the secured area (You won’t have chance to check the token value again once you close the prompt window)

4. Build Connection in SAC

a. Go to Connection are in SAC -> Create New Connection -> Search for SQL Database

b. Select correct LocationID and Generic JDBC datasource

Give a meaningful name for the connection (the name can be update later) > Enter JDBC URL > Enter JDBC Class > Enter User Name = token > Enter Password = Access token > (Optional) Select Share credentials when sharing this connection > Click on Create button


Note: If you want to share the credential details, select the option Share these credentials when sharing this connection. Otherwise, users will need to enter their own credentials in order to use the connection. If you don't share your credentials, users will be able to edit their credentials at any time without having to start a data acquisition process.

5. Test Connection

Conclusion

Congratulations on completing the setup of your JDBC connection between SAP Analytics Cloud and Databricks. By following this guide, you have successfully configured your SAC connection to Databricks. With this integration, you can now leverage the powerful analytics and data visualization capabilities of SAC using data stored in Databricks. We hope this guide has been helpful and has made the setup process straightforward and efficient. If you have any questions or need further assistance, feel free to reach out.

entry.title: Me salvó la timidez
entry.content:
Hace un tiempo me preguntaron esto:

cual es tu mayor debilidad? Me quede pasmada por un segundo, pero había leído que cuando eso pasa debes respirar 5 segundos para que el oxigeno llegue a tu cerebro y te tranquilices, entonces lo  hice y una brillante idea llego a mi:

Dije ; "Mi mayor debilidad es la timidez, soy muy tímida"  el entrevistador me miró asombrado y yo agregue,

" Pero empecé un curso hace un mes para vencer la timidez y hablar en publico y me ha funcionado de maravilla, míreme aquí sentada frente a usted con toda la tranquilidad aplicando buenas tácticas para hablar en público"

Les cuento que llevo 16 años trabajando aquí, liderando procesos de SAP y por supuesto hablando en público de maravilla!

Saludos!

entry.title: How to display full screen picture in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am developing a mobile app using MDK.

I have to display several pictures from the backend.

I have managed to implement the OData interface for that.

The picture is displayed in the ImageCollection in the SectionedTable using online OData request:

"Controls": [
    {
      "Sections": [
         {
          "ImageCell": {
            "Image": "/SAPAssetManager/Services/OnlineAssetManager.service/{@odata.readLink}/$value",          
            "Title": "{@odata.readLink}"
          },
          "Layout": {
            "LayoutType": "Vertical",
            "NumberOfColumns": 1
          },  
          "Target": {
            "EntitySet": "ZPictures",
            "Service": "/SAPAssetManager/Services/OnlineAssetManager.service",

entry.title: Where can I start learning the billing and invoicing process?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Contract Accounting & Invoicing learning journey

Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the generic billing, invoicing and accounting processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Contract Accounting and Invoicing, within the context of SAP Solutions for Quote to Cash Management public cloud (BRIM and CPQ).

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, consultants will be able to execute the billing and invoicing process and gain an overview of the Revenue Sharing and Partner Settlement. In addition, you will learn to describe and execute the integration of Billing and Invoicing with Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable and General Ledger Accounting. The use of both transactional and, most importantly analytical Fiori Apps for Convergent Invoicing and Contract Accounting is covered.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: how to manage different custom mappings for same docu...
entry.content:
While performing Custom Mapping we have a provision to provide system ID for which the mapping applies to. You can leverage this option to maintain different mapping for ECC and S4.

In case you have mapping for ECC alone, mention the system ID of ECC while performing custom mapping and deploy it. 

In actual run and based on custom routing, system ID is determined and the custom mapping performed for that specific system ID will be utilized.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: Adding new tabs in Manage Purchase Requisitions Profe...
entry.content:
Hello customer, 

To add a new section, please follow this documentation: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/a357047be956436ebb1dfebf1aa29af2.html

You cannot add a section using 'Custom Fields & Logic app' or a BAdI. You need to add a fragment extension.
It's recommended that you create a custom app if you want to add your customization. In case of any bugs, it would be easier to check in the future if they are caused by SAP standard code or your own code. In addition, you may need to adapt your custom code each time you upgrade the system.

Adding only custom fields via 'Custom Fields & Logic app' is the recommended approach. 

Best regards, 
Tatiana

entry.title: Re: How to display full screen picture in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
The Image Collection is designed to show a thumbnail view of an image along with optional labels.

If you want to display an image to fill the width of the page you should consider using the Image Section which will give you options on how large the image will render.  However if you need multiple images you will need an image section per image to be displayed.

entry.title: Re: Converting a SAP Fiori “Manage” app to a Display Only using Adapt UI
entry.content:
Thanks Colleen. This is the exact issue we are facing with, as the security model from SAP GUI for display-only roles doesn't quite work in Fiori.

But imo, the Enterprise Search is not really a good user experience. Fiori Launchpad is supposed to show users exactly what they need to do for their job. It would be bad usability to expect users to use tiles for some tasks, and for others remember to do a Search. Also, the Manage tiles delivered by SAP have many good features that users will miss if we don't give them the Manage tiles (eg. graphical process map for sales order fullfilment). 

Adapt UI and creating runtime variants seems to be the only effective way to provide display-only version of apps, without breaking Fiori user experience principles,

Thanks

entry.title: Re: EDI 856 ASN Outbound to Customer from GR document
entry.content: Not sure how you would generate an ASN (outbound) from the recording of an inbound action like a statistical GR. Any typical 856 would be recorded against either a delivery (or possibly a shipment), and generated against that transaction data accordingly. If you were to venture into that space you would need to generate all the data associations that are required by way of a logical mapping or a custom business document of sorts. There would exist certain data points that you would be unable to reflect too... e.g. how would you record a carrier for a GR? A GR is a record of a physical movement whereas a delivery is a business document that logically organizes all activities required to get particular goods out the door.

entry.title: SAC Excel MEMERPROPERTY not returning Description
entry.content:
I have a number of Excel files setup using the SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY function to label rows and columns.
Today I am finding that previously working cells now give me a #VALUE! error in Excel with the SAC Excel Add-in.

For example:
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Measures", "qty_u", "Description")
This used to return the Description of my measure "Unit Qty" - today I get #VALUE!
=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Measures", "qty_u", "Name")
Still returns "qty_u" as it previously did

=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Version", "public.2024FC", "firstPlanDate")
works and returns my custom property as defined in the dimension tables in the modeler

=SAP.MEMBERPROPERTY(DS_1, "Version", "public.2024FC", "Description")
used to return "2024FC" but now results in a #VALUE! error in excel

 

Has something changed? SAC update? Add-in Update?

What am I missing here - being able to pull in the Description from my Account dimensions, measures, versions, etc. was a huge time savings in some report setups I have been using for several months.

Thanks

entry.title: NEW PDF Executive Summary now delivered by default with each discovery edition delivery
entry.content:
If you wish to get an introduction to the PDF Executive Summary, you can read this blog.

 

Dear Community,

We are excited to announce that the PDF Executive Summary is now included by default with each delivery of SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition.

The summary, attached directly to the delivery email, replaces the previous Getting Started Guide to streamline content delivery and ensure you receive all necessary information without overwhelming attachments.

Additionally, we have expanded the content by incorporating two new topics:

  • AI recommendations
  • HR recommendations

These enhancements aim to foster more in-depth innovation and transformation discussions with your customers, focusing on high-visibility and critical topics.

Learn in detail what’s New in the PDF Executive Summary:

AI Recommendations

For each Line of Business, the new "Business AI Recommendations" section has been added to the level 3 Navigation bar:

When you click on it you will find a table with the AI recommendations listed by Value Drivers and PPI:

On the right side of the table (highlighted in red), you can find the name and the type of the AI recommendations for the Line of Business. By clicking on it, you will be provided with more information. (View the Innovation type legend on the bottom).

By looking at the left part of the table you can see what value driver and which PPI (Process Performance Indicator) this innovation relates to.

(You can click on each Value Driver or PPI to review the system information online).

And just next to the PPI column, you can review the value extracted from the system for this indicator (the highest the value is the most potential of the AI recommendation).

If there is information in the Value Analysis (online) for the related indicator, it will also be displayed:


This allows you to directly determine the potential monetary impact on process performance from implementing an AI recommendation. Please note that each Innovations related to the same Value Driver/PPI will be listed together.

For the same PPI, the other cells of the table will be empty:



HR Recommendations

Please note: We did not develop any Process Performance indicator nor any other SAP Signavio capability. We are mapping HR solutions (SuccessFactors, Concur) to used ERP or SAP S/4HANA transactions.

We brought back the HR recommendations from Process Discovery (now discontinued).

We still have the 3 pages: Introduction, Recommendations list, and getting started:

The introduction (on the left highlighted in blue are the HR areas currently used in the source system):

The recommendations list (similar to what you will find in the SAP S/4HANA recommendations):

And the recommended “Next Steps”: The SAP Customer Evolution Kit for HXM and the SAP Readiness Check for HCM, with links to more information and register:

Note: The content is NOT accessible via the “Lines of Business” section of the PDF (because we do not have performance metrics and deep dive capabilities for this area).

To access the HR content, you will need to go to the “Next Steps” section of the top navigation bar, select “ERP Transformation” from the second level navigation and “Additional Human Resources Content”.

Request your free* SAP Signavio Process, Insights discovery edition analysis today: https://url.sap/discovery-edition
(*For SAP maintenance customers)

In case you have any questions feel free to leave a comment in the comment section or reach out via email to: Signavio-Discovery@sap.com.

Kind regards
SAP Signavio Discovery Edition

entry.title: Configuring country specific Rehire Verification Process
entry.content:
Introduction:

Rehiring is the process where organizations hire their former employees again back to their organization. When it happens, the former employees as an external candidates get hired across legal entities where they worked as an employee before. Since the candidates can be from different Legal entities from different countries, it is good to assign their Rehiring process to the country specific Onboarding Manager groups as similar as country specific Onboarding process to maintain the process as simple as Onboarding process for Onboarding Managers.

Onboarding supports this process by assigning the Rehire verification process to the country specific Onboarding Managers to carry forward with the Rehiring process.

Pre-requisites:

  1. Configuring Rehire check.
  2. Enabling permission to Rehire Inactive Employee (by ‘match’ in New Hire) and Rehire Inactive Employee with New Employment (by ‘match’ in New Hire) (as per the requirement).
  3. Creating Rehire Coordinator responsible group or repurposing the Country specific Onboarding Manager groups (as per the requirement).
  4. Configure Rehire Coordinator Business rule.
  5. Enabling Rehire with Old employment switch in Onboarding General settings.

Note: Onboarding HR Managers who are going to review the Rehire verification process, must have required permission for external candidate’s Personal Information and Job information. Mostly, Onboarding Managers who review New Hire data must be having the permission already.

For more information about the pre-requisite and overview about Rehire with Old and New employment: Rehire with SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding: From Exit to Re-entry 

 

Configuration for Country specific Rehire verification process:

  • Create a country specific Rehire verification group for Onboarding Managers.

Note: You can reuse the country specific Onboarding Managers group that are already created for country specific Onboarding process for this Rehire assignment rule or else we can create a country specific group for Rehire verification process specifically.

Responsible group

  •  Creating a business rule

To assign the Rehire verification process, create a business rule that validates the country of a legal entity for which the candidate is getting hired for and then assign that country specific Onboarding Managers group like given in the screen shot.

 Business rule configuration

  •  Configure the business rule in Onboarding configuration:

Once the rule is created, assign this rule to get trigger.

Navigate to Manage Data → Search Onboarding Configuration → Select the Default Object → Search for - Rule for Rehire Coordinator Group Configuration and assign the rule in the field → Save.

Recruit/Rehire Configuration

Expected result:

After the configuration setup, once the onboarding is initiated for the candidate, system validates whether the candidate is a Rehire or not. If the candidate is a Rehire, system assigns the Rehire verification process to that country specific Onboarding HR Managers for the Profile Verification Process.

Example:

Onboarding Manager added to ONB HR Manager USA group.

 Rehire Verification Process is assigned to the same Onboarding HR Manager who is part of USA group.

 

 

 

I hope this document is clear and informative, I would like to hear your thoughts and feedback on this topic. So, feel free to share your thoughts in the comments. 

Thanks for reading this document!

entry.title: Re: LLM Fine-tuning
entry.content:
Nice article.

The SAP solution for ABAP code is incorrect though. I'm in S/4HANA 2022 system and IF_FIN_RE_DATA_CACHE method CONTAINS doesn't have ER_DATA in the signature. Which makes sense because based on the method's name, I'd expect it to return just true/false value. There is another method (GET), shown in the screenshot, which I'd expect to provide the data.

It's possible that whatever system this screenshot is from could have a different method signature (and ChatGPT didn't get a memo on this). But again, based on the name, I can't think of any reason to do that.

Not to mention if I'm correct about the method's signature (it just needs to check whether a record exists), then I'd add TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS for better performance. And SAP added all that cryptic new syntax like VALUE etc. and now your AI is not using it and uses READ TABLE? The air is thick with irony here. 🙂

Bottom line, I wouldn't rush to declare a victory just yet.

entry.title: Simple S4HANA EWM Inbound process steps with warehouse process type configuration
entry.content:
Introduction:

Let us see the simple configuration required for doing Inbound delivery in Embedded EWM. Prerequisites are maintained in the Blog 

Process Flow:

Below is the process flow - We create the Purchase order and Inbound delivery in ERP system. Against which an Inbound delivery will be created in the EWM system automatically. Then we need to create a warehouse task and move the material from door (ground) and put it in the respective bin (Rack).

Main Part.

Overview:

  1. Organization Structure
  2. Configurations
  3. Master data
  4. Transactional data - Process flow

Organization structure:

I have created one plant 0075, WH 075 in ERP and Location 0075, WH 0075 (1 extra zero) in EWM.

In Which I have two storage locations AFS and ROD. These two storage locations are connected to the warehouse and this will create the Inbound delivery in EWM when you create IBD in ERP. This storage location needs to be maintained in the Purchase order

Also, I have Six storage types that come under warehouse and it comes under EWM in the system.

ROD - Received on DOC. This storage location contains the raw materials from the purchase. After receiving the material from the supplier we will place the materials in ROD.

AFS - Available for sale. Usually, these storage locations contain the finished materials from the production planning which are ready for sale.

Storage Types and Bins.

DOOR - This storage type I have created for Door. Bins are DRGR, DRGI and created via /n/SCWM/LS01.

SAIB - This storage type I have created for GR - Inbound deliveries. Bin is SAIB (Not used here)

SAOB - This storage type I have created for GI - Outbound SAOB. Bins is SAOB (Not used here)

ROD1 - This storage type I have created for rack system and its like ROD storage location where the raw materials are kept. Bins are from ROD1-01-01-01 to ROD1-01-04-03 and created via /n/SCWM/LS10.

AFS1 - This storage type I have created for rack system and its like AFS storage location where the finished materials are kept. Bin is AFS1-01-01-01 (Not used here)

Process

In our process, I am not using any special storage process. I am going to receive the material in storage type DOOR / bin DRGR and going to put it in WH storage type ROD1 / bin ROD1-01-01-01 by selecting the bin manually in EWM inbound order.

Configuration:

Created a warehouse process type.

All warehouse process types are further grouped into warehouse process categories. Warehouse in to warehouse process categories. Warehouse process categories are used to define the goods movement in a warehouse.

There are different warehouse process categories that are predefined in the EWM system.

  • Stock removal

  • Put-away
  • Internal Movement
  • Goods receipt posting
  • Goods issue postings

This will decide the process step - Either this flow is IB or OB or IM related. For more check link 

IMG - SCM Extended Warehouse Management - Extended Warehouse Management - Cross-Process Settings - Warehouse Task - Define Warehouse Process Type.

Copied one standard EWM warehouse process type 'PTWY' for new WH 0075 as below

Here the source storage type I used is DOOR and bin as DRGR. So that when ever the IB created in EWM the material will be first received in bin DRGR. Also the WH process category I used is 1 - Putaway.

Configure - Determine Warehouse Process Type:

IMG - SCM Extended Warehouse Management - Extended Warehouse Management - Cross-Process Settings - Warehouse Task - Determine Warehouse Process Type.

Assigned the newly created Warehouse process type 'PTWY' to Inbound documents in WH 0075. Now this WPT will be used all the time for INB documents.

Master data:

Material Master:

Created a material master 'EWMA01' in MM01 with purchasing view and in ERP WH 07

Created the same material in EWM system via /n/SCWM/MAT1. You can use the existing PED.

Saved it at the end.

Transaction data or Running the process:

Created Purchase order - ME21N

Note: Here you need to maintain the confirmation indicator (to create IB in VL31N) and EWM storage location (to generate the IB in EWM). PO - 4500000204

Created Inbound document in ERP - VL31N

Enterd the PO in VL31N

Saved the IBD in next screen

If all the configuration are in place then below success message will be displayed

Doing GR for INB document in EWM system. - /N/SCWM/PRDI

Put the IBD delivery 180000101 create from previous step and execute. Here click Goods receipt to receive the material in door.

At item level click the highlighted details button to get the receiving BIN and WPT which we configured.

Now go to Inbound delivery > follow on function > Warehouse task on top of same screen to create a warehouse task.

In next screen select the line item, click on default values at item level and maintain the destination bin as ROD-01-01-01 manually, Click create and save it

Now go to /N/SCWM/MON. Where go to inbound documents and click execute. Then select the INB and click warehouse task on top. Then click the details button at bottom to get the destination bin.

Now confirm the warehouse task in background

You will get a success message

Now you can see the stock in respective bin RA01-01-01-01 in physical stock.

But in EWM inbound delivery you could see the documents types. The mapping are done in configuration.

Document types at header level mapping in EWM - /N/SCWM/PRDI

In header if you click the details button, you could see that the document category and types.

The header level ERP vs EWM document mapping are found under below configuration

SCM Extended Warehouse Management - Extended Warehouse Management - Interfaces - ERP Integration - Delivery Processing - Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

SCM Extended Warehouse Management - Extended Warehouse Management - Goods Receipt Process - Inbound Delivery - Define Document Type Determination for Inbound Delivery Process - Define Document Types for Inbound Delivery Process

In ERP in VL33N you can get the header document type

Document types at Item level mapping in EWM

At Item level you can see the document types in IBD

At item level the ERP vs EWM document mapping are found under below configuration

SCM Extended Warehouse Management - Extended Warehouse Management - Interfaces - ERP Integration - Delivery Processing - Map Item Types from ERP System to EWM

In ERP VL33N you can get this doc type.

Below that you can see some status.

The status profiles are maintained in below IMG

SCM Extended Warehouse Management < Extended Warehouse Management > Cross-Process Settings > Delivery - Warehouse Request > Status Management > Define Status Profiles

Conclusion:

Hope this may support you on understanding the basic Inbound process in embedded EWM

Please write below if you have any comments on it.

entry.title: Driving Efficiency and Growth with an SAP Business Network Supplier Account
entry.content:
There is more technology available today than ever before in history. Technology is growing at an exponential rate, and businesses are forced to keep up or get left behind in the fiercely competitive global economy. What’s important not to forget amidst the buzz and excitement around some of the current technology trends in business (you know what I’m talking about) is the fundamental digital elements that help businesses bring out their best. In a hyper-connected and interdependent ecosystem, no business does business alone. Therefore, it is crucial that businesses have the technology in place to be able to collaborate efficiently and effectively with other businesses. 

Read our supplier’s insightful perspective on change in business. 

What is SAP Business Network? 

SAP Business Network modernizes how procurement and supply chain processes are connected across companies. It closes the break point between buyers and suppliers by enabling document collaboration with configurable business rules, data sharing, and trading partner search and discovery. This helps companies gain greater visibility into their supply chain, increase operational efficiency, and ensure compliance. 

What is an SAP Business Network supplier account? 

To join and access SAP Business Network, organizations looking to sell can sign up for a standard account for free. This account enables suppliers to 

  • digitally exchange documents and collaborate with their customers, 
  • get discovered by new customers, 
  • receive leads and participate in sourcing events, 
  • have greater visibility into their transactions, 
  • optimize their business for compliance with customer requirements, 
  • manage their accounts,  
  • and customize their experience. 

Who should use a standard account? 

Any organization can sign up in just a few minutes for an account and begin using it. It is an excellent resource to expand your global reach, especially for suppliers who are looking for business opportunities to grow their business and get discovered by new customers to develop business relationships. 

Size:  

The standard account is ideal for companies of all sizes, ranging from small to medium-sized businesses (SMB) all the way to large enterprises.  

Industries:  

SAP Business Network spans many industries, including but not limited to products and services in the following industries: 

  • Manufacturing 
  • Professional Services 
  • Retail 
  • Finance & Insurance 
  • Construction 
  • Medical 
  • Construction & Maintenance Services 
  • Computer Hardware, Software & Telecom 
  • And many others 

Why use an SAP Business Network supplier account / standard account? 

A supplier account on SAP Business Network truly has all the features you need to successfully collaborate with your customers and grow your business. 

Business Outcome 

Feature 

Streamline business processes 

  • Digitize transactions and reduce errors with e-invoicing and electronic order management for an unlimited number of documents 
  • Manage multiple customers in one place 

Enhance customer collaboration 

  • Message customers about documents; messages are then automatically embedded in the document 
  • Receive critical updates from customers through buyer announcements 

Improve visibility over key business insights 

  • Easy access to important information, such as document and payment status and key performance indicators (KPI) analytics in one place 

Optimize for compliance with customer requirements 

  • Automatically validate orders and invoices (based on buyer-configured rules) 
  • Demonstrate compliance with legal and customer requirements with the standardized human rights self-assessment 

Customizable user experience 

  • Customize the tools available based on your business needs with account management features  
  • Customizable homepage 
  • Access to SAP Business Network Supplier mobile app to keep up with orders on-the-go 

Greater discoverability 

  • Suppliers can get discovered by interested buyers with a searchable company profile that includes sustainability ratings and certifications, product keywords, and operational locations 

More business opportunities 

  • Grow your business and build long-term customer relationships in a thriving global network of trading partners by participating in sourcing events and receiving new leads from SAP Business Network Discovery postings 

 

If you are not a member of SAP Business Network already, sign up today for a world of possibilities for your business!

entry.title: SAC - Signflip in Calculation of Previous Years Values
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

we have a problem in SAP Analytics Cloud, data modeling with the Signflip of EXP-Accounts. 

Situation

We have an Account-Dimension with a Sign-Flip-Property, which should enable different signs for the EXP-Accounts (Sign Flip for Base Amount shows the Costs as a positive value / Sign Flip for Management shows Costs as a negative Amount). 

This works well, as decribed in the Documentation (SAP Help Portal). Also for the Calculation (Row 3 - Calculation of "Direct Margin = Revenue - Direct Cost of Sales") it works fine, after setting the right Priorities for the Calculation. Exmaple see Screenshot below. 

Problem

We need to calculate the Previous Year Values. But here the Signflip "destroys" the calculation when it comes to the time navigation. 

The first 2 Columns (green) are correct ans also the result (Direct Margin) is calculated correctly.

  • LC (no Signflip) shows always positive Values
  • LC shows Cost as negative Values

In the last Column (red) we can see the problem for the previous Year Calculation:

  • LC - (PY) does show the Cost as negative value, but the calculation of Direct Margin is wrong (it adds the 2 Accounts above). I would expect 2,960k - 2,178k = 782k as a direct margin. Like in the middle column for LC. 

Background

New Model Type, optimized Story. 

LC (no Signflip) - is the Measure in the Import-Model. 

LC - is a calculated Measure to reflect the signflip:

 

LC - (PY) - is a calculated measure to get the previous year value from LC (only Actuals):

 

It would be great if someone can help here! 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to restrict the access to truly display for some applications?
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

 

We have a role containing tile 'My Purchase Requisitions' and other containing tile 'Display Purchase requisitions'.

When we assign both the roles to a user, user is able to edit all the purchase requisitions. Is there a way, we can restrict the access to truly display with both roles assigned to a single user?

 

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict the access to truly display for some...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

In order to provide display access for both applications, you can restrict the Business Roles with "Write Access: No Access".

 

The authorization concept for business roles is based on the concept of granting authorizations (as opposed to denying them). By adding business catalogs to a business role and assigning the business role to a business user, you can control which applications the business user is authorized to carry out. As a next step, you define how the customer data can be accessed.

In the Maintain Restrictions UI, you can maintain restrictions for business roles. These restrictions are the basis of authorizations that are granted to the business users who are assigned to this business role. The business catalog defines which access categories are available for maintaining and for which fields restriction values can be maintained.

The following access categories are available:
  • Write, Read, Value Help (write access)
  • Read, Value Help (read access)
  • Value Help (value help access)

If you set the "Write, read, value help" to "No access", this means that the role will be read only and therefore, the users will not be able to edit any data.

 

For more information, please check SAP Help Portal | How to Define Authorizations Based on Restrictions

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: In MD07 traffic siginal, What is definition of 1st Re...
entry.content:
It is number of days the available stock can satisfy the demand qty .

Stock Day’s supply Calculation = On Hand Stock - Demand

1st Receipt Day’s Supply : On hand Stock + Open PO - Demand

2nd Receipt day’s supply  : On hand stock + Open PO + Firmed Purchase requisition – Demand

We can include any receipt type in customization for calculation of 1st RDS & 2nd RDS.

entry.title: Re: Procedure cached plans not reused for new connection
entry.content:
Some assumptions:

  • a new connection/spid always sees the first invocation of the proc taking 15 seconds; all subsequent calls (by the same spid) always run fast (~300 ms)
  • after the 15-second initial proc call, a query of monCachedProcedures shows the PlanID and CompileDate change and the ExecutionCount is reset to 0; (if there are multiple rows in monCachedProcedures for this proc then I would expect only one of these rows to see said changes after a 15-second proc call)
  • once you get past the 15-second initial proc call (ie, all follow-on proc calls are fast @ ~300 ms), all repeated queries of monCachedProcedures show that PlanID and CompileDate columns are not changing while the ExecutionCount column shows an ever increasing/incrementing value
  • you are using the same ASE version on the old (non-azure) instance and the new (azure) instance; or if using different ASE versions the differences are in the minor/subversions (ie, we're not comparing ASE 12.5.4 vs ASE 15, or ASE 15 vs ASE 16)

--------------------------

re: the lengthy 15-second run time for the 'initial' proc call

The initial lengthy proc call is likely related to an excessive (re)compile time, with subsequent calls being (relatively) fast due to not having to recompile the proc.

But why aren't you seeing this issue on the old (non-azure) instance?

Off the top of my head I'm guessing a difference in optimizer related configuration settings could be at play here with the primary culprit being optimization goal; consider:

  • allrows_oltp - enables the fewest optimizer features which in turn can mean the (re)compile phase is (relatively) fast
  • allrows_mix [default config] - enables a few more optimizer features (eg, merge joins) which in turn can mean the (re)compile phase takes longer (than allrows_oltp); with complicated, multi-table queries it's not uncommon to see the (re)compile phase take many seconds, or even minutes, to complete
  • allrows_dss - enables a few more optimizer features (eg, merge and hash joins) which in turn can mean the (re)compile phase may (still) take longer (than allrows_oltp) to complete; again, total (re)compile time will be based on the complexity of the query(s) in the proc

My best guess on what's happening:

  • the old (non-azure) instance is configured with optimization goal == allrows_oltp
  • the new (azure) instance is configured with optimization goal == allrows_mix (or allrows_dss)
  • the (re)compile process is occurring in both (non-azure, azure) instances but the faster allrows_oltp based (re)compilation is so (relatively) fast that users don't see any noticeable differences in proc speeds

While this could explain the proc calls that take 15 seconds it doesn't explain why all spids are are incurring a 15 second run time the first time each spid calls the proc; spids that find an available entry in procedure cache should not incur the 15 second delay.

FWIW:

  • To this day I still recommend clients configure their ASE instances to use optimization goal == allrows_oltp if simply because the extra compilation overhead of allrows_mix/allrows_dss is still quite excessive; for queries where merge and/or hash joins would be of benefit I recommend clients enable/disable connection level settings as needed or use abstract query plans.
  • It hasn't been mentioned (so far) in this Q&A so I'll throw out the (obvious) recommendation ... conduct a detailed comparison of the configuration settings (sp_configure) for both (non-azure, azure) instances to insure you're comparing apples to apples.

The next guess would be a change in the application's connection process and/or a change in an ASE login trigger/proc that's changing some compiler related configs at the connection level.  Short of doing some code reviews you could try running sp_options show, null, null, <spid> against processes running on both (non-azure, azure) instances to see if there are any differences in compiler settings at the connection levels.

--------------------------

A couple scenarios come to mind where #temp tables can wreak havoc on performance by forcing excessive (re)compile operations

  • (sp_configure) optimize temp table resolution is set to the default setting of 0/off (default): when set to 0/off the proc (re)compiles are based on the #temp table object id so multiple spids, creating the same #temp table (but with difference object ids) and then calling the same proc (that references the #temp table), will see recompiles due to the differences in #temp table object ids; when set to 1/on the proc recompiles are typically reduced, if not eliminated, because the differences in object ids are ignored; this should also apply for #temp tables created across multiple tempdbs
  • regardless of how optimize temp table resolution is configured you can still see proc recompiles if the different spids have #temp tables with the same name but a difference in the structure of the table, eg, different sets of columns, different order of columns (in the create table call), different datatypes, different indexes

One (obvious) problem with this idea (#temp table related recompilations) is it would not be limited to just the initial proc call (for a given spid); in other words, issues with #temp table related recompilations should be occurring quite frequently throughout the life of a connection/spid where multiple spids are (re)using the same entry from procedure cache

--------------------------

An additional idea that builds on the idea of proc recompilations based on #temp table structure changes and which could explain why the only recompilation occurs with a spid's initial proc call:

  • spid creates a #temp table with no indexes
  • spid calls proc (first time)
  • proc checks to see if the #temp table has a specific index; if the index does not exist the proc creates the index
  • the creation of the index is considered a change in the #temp table's structure so the proc is recompiled
  • proc completes and control is returned to the parent spid
  • at this point the #temp table has a new index
  • spid calls proc again
  • proc checks to see if the #temp table has a specific index; this index exists so no new index needs to be created which in turn means the #temp table's structure does not change which means the proc does not have to be recompiled
  • all other spids reusing this proc (from procedure cache) also already have the (new) index so no need to perform the create-index/change-table-structure/recompile-proc phase

A variation on this idea would see the conditional create index replaced with a conditional update statistics call.

--------------------------

You've mentioned in a comment the proc uses a global temporary table. I would expect a global temporary table to be treated more like a permanent table (as opposed to a #temp table) if simply because we're dealing with a single object id and a fixed structure; all I've got at this point are questions

  • are you using global temporary tables in the old (non-azure) instance
  • are you by any chance creating multiple global temporary tables (same name but in different tempdb databases)
  • is one instance using a single tempdb while the other instance is using multiple tempdbs

 

Assuming this isn't an issue with the optimization goal configuration, and there are no performance issues with global temporary tables in the old (non-azure) instance, but there are differences (code changes, introduction of multi-tempdb databases) in the new (azure) instance then I'd probably opt for opening a tech support case to see if there any known issues with your 'new' environment.

entry.title: Re: F1520 Proposed Posting Date Field during Reprocessing...
entry.content:
Hi,

Have you read the restriction on the note itself, it clearly states:

  1. If there is provided a value for the posting date field in the bank statement during the import via the format mapping or manual creation, this value is proposed as the first option on the detail screen of a bank statement item.
  2. The first option as an alternative default value, the application proposes the value of the bank statement date in the posting date field on the detail screen of a bank statement item.
  3. When the item is edited and saved as a draft with a different value in the posting date field, this new value is kept and proposed instead of the bank statement date or another value from the bank statement format mapping within this field.

Have you followed this?

If yes and you saved the draft with a different value in the posting date field and it does not take it over, I would propose you open an incident.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: R47 Delete ABAP Session after Error
entry.content:
Hello,

Does the Dispatcher trace (dev_disp) log anything about the terminal ID (T208_U1004_M0 at the original post), around that same timestamp?
